checks.a_analysis: I have compared the output JSON against the source PDF page by page. All paragraphs and list items in the JSON correspond directly to content found in the source document. There are no fabricated sections, introductions, or conclusions.
The source document contains one email address: PSAPeligibilityrequest@nc.gov. I verified this against the JSON output. The JSON correctly includes this address with its corresponding mailto: link.

The JSON output contains all information from both pages of the source PDF. It correctly bridges the text that spans the break between page 1 and page 2 (the paragraph starting 'If requesting eligibility for maintenance...').
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, this check is not applicable and is marked as PASS.

Formatting choices such as bolding and italics (e.g., for the July 1, 2025 date and the disclaimer paragraph) have been faithfully captured in the JSON structure. No words or numbers have been altered.

checks.a_analysis: The JSON output was reviewed for any repeating blocks of text that appear only once in the source PDF. Each paragraph and list item from the source appears exactly once in the semantic output. There is no evidence of duplicated content or repeated sections.

The main title 'PSAP Emergency Telephone System Fund (ETSF) Eligibility Procedure' is correctly assigned as the section title. The document structure is simple and flat. The lead-in text for the final list ('Preliminary Questions...') is rendered as a paragraph, which is a standard interpretation and does not break any hierarchical rules as there are no subsequent headings to skip to.
The source document does not contain any tables. The JSON output correctly reflects this with a 'table_count' of 0 and no table elements within the sections.

The reading order in the JSON follows the logical flow of the source PDF. Specifically, the paragraph starting at the bottom of page 1 ('If requesting eligibility for maintenance...') and concluding on page 2 ('...apply to maintenance costs)') has been correctly merged into a single paragraph element, maintaining the document's intended flow.

The document contains two distinct lists. The first list (indicated by diamond bullets in the source) is correctly identified as an 'unordered' list with 4 items. The second list (numbered 1-6 in the source) is correctly identified as an 'ordered' list. All list items are correctly split and maintain their meaning.

The email address 'PSAPeligibilityrequest@nc.gov' present in the text on page 1 has been correctly converted into a 'mailto:' link. No other links are present in the source, and no additional links were fabricated in the output.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the HTML output matches the source PDF perfectly. The document structure flows from the title to the introductory paragraphs, followed by the bulleted list requirements, and concluding with the numbered 'Preliminary Questions' section. Although page breaks are removed in the HTML version, the logical sequence and visual placement of sections are consistent with the source document's intent.
The source PDF does not contain any tables. No table elements were expected or found in the HTML output.
The source PDF is entirely text-based and contains no meaningful images, logos, or diagrams. The HTML correctly reflects this absence of graphical content.

A careful review of the HTML screenshot shows no instances of duplicated body paragraphs, list items, or sections. The transition between content from Page 1 and Page 2 of the PDF occurs seamlessly without repetition.
